title: Atomic Habits
author: James Clear
description: An easy and proven way to build good habits and break bad ones. James Clear reveals exactly how these microscopic changes can grow into life-altering outcomes.

title: Clean Code
author: Robert C. Martin
description: Even bad code can function. But if code isn't clean, it can bring a development organization to its knees. This book is a must-read for any software engineer.

title: The Psychology of Money
author: Morgan Housel
description: Doing well with money isn't necessarily about what you know. It's about how you behave. And behavior is hard to teach, even to really smart people.

title: Deep Work
author: Cal Newport
description: Rules for focused success in a distracted world. Deep work is the ability to focus without distraction on a cognitively demanding task.

title: You Don't Know JS Yet
author: Kyle Simpson
description: The world-famous book series on JavaScript. Dive deep into the mechanics of the language and understand how it truly works under the hood.

title: Thinking, Fast and Slow
author: Daniel Kahneman
description: The famous Nobel laureate Daniel Kahneman explores the two systems that drive the way we think — System 1 (fast, intuitive) and System 2 (slow, logical).

title: The Pragmatic Programmer
author: Andrew Hunt, David Thomas
description: Your journey to mastery. One of the most significant books in the field of software development, updated for the 20th anniversary.

title: Start with Why
author: Simon Sinek
description: How great leaders inspire everyone to take action. Simon Sinek explores the concept of the 'Golden Circle' and why some organizations are more innovative.

title: Zero to One
author: Peter Thiel
description: Notes on startups, or how to build the future. Peter Thiel explores how to create things that are truly new and transition from 0 to 1.

title: The Lean Startup
author: Eric Ries
description: How today's entrepreneurs use continuous innovation to create radically successful businesses. The foundation of modern startup methodology.

title: Sapiens
author: Yuval Noah Harari
description: A brief history of humankind. Harari surveys the history of humankind from the evolution of archaic human species in the Stone Age up to the twenty-first century.

title: Refactoring
author: Martin Fowler
description: Improving the design of existing code. A comprehensive guide to refactoring patterns and techniques for better software maintainability.
